title.rendered: Meeting Across the River: Bruce Springsteen and Joe Grushecky, Pumping Iron

content.rendered:
In 1980, Joe Grushecky’s band, the Iron City Houserockers, were recording their second album, Have a Good Time But Get Out Alive.

Steve Van Zandt produced and arranged five of the songs on that album (and played guitar on one), all while still working in the studio with the E Street Band on The River. One night, Steve invited Joe to tag along and introduced him to Bruce. That, as they say, was the beginning of a long and beautiful friendship.

One of the songs that Steve did not have a hand in but still ended up on the album was “Pumping Iron,” as much an anthem for Pittsburgh as anything Bruce ever wrote for New Jersey.

“Pumping Iron” couldn’t be more perfectly tailored for a Grushecky/Springsteen team-up, which is probably why they’ve performed it together almost two dozen times over the years (and probably more to come).

One the earliest of those performances was captured live on October 20, 1995, at Nick’s Fat City in Pittsburgh on the American Babylon Tour. It was officially released on Joe’s Down the Road Apiece live album in 1999.

Take a listen to this superb recording of Joe Grushecky and The Houserockers (featuring Bruce Springsteen) performing “Pumping Iron.”

Bruce joined Joe and The Houserockers throughout their American Babylon Tour (otherwise known as the October Assault), and “Pumping Iron” featured prominently in the setlist.

Bonus: Bruce and Joe would reprise it many times over the years that followed–although only in Pittsburgh or Asbury Park. Their last performances (to date, at least) were at the Wonder Bar on July 18, 2015, and at Light of Day in January 2020, and fortunately they were captured from close-up.

Pumping Iron
First performed: July 22, 1995 (Sea Bright, NJ)
Last performed: January 18, 2020 (Asbury Park, NJ)

excerpt.rendered: As much an anthem for Pittsburgh as anything Bruce ever wrote for New Jersey, “Pumping Iron” is a surefire setlist bet any time Joe Grushecky and Bruce play together.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Have a Good Time… But Get Out Alive!
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The album that led to a legendary lifelong friendship gets a 40th anniversary reissue today. Let's take a listen and look back on Joe Grushecky and the Iron City Houserockers' watershed album.

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR: Joe Grushecky and Bruce Springsteen, Junior’s Bar
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins Joe Grushecky on one of the Iron City Houserockers’ best early tracks, "Junior's Bar."

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR: Joe Grushecky and Bruce Springsteen: East Carson Street
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce plays guitar on the title track of Joe Grushecky's 2009 album, but even without Bruce's contribution, "East Carson Street" is a great song about staying true to one's roots.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Meeting Across the River: Joe Grushecky and Bruce Springsteen, Never Be Enough Time
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Never Be Enough Time" is a standout track from a stellar album, and a joyous collaboration between Joe Grushecky and Bruce.

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR: Joe Grushecky and Bruce Springsteen: Talking to the King
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Talking to the King" is a sweet, count-your-blessings rocker and a ton of fun in concert. No wonder Joe and Bruce have played it together 28 times. Check out some of the best inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR: Joe Grushecky and Bruce Springsteen, “Lighten Up”
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A one-off performance of Joe Gruchecky's "Lighten Up" with Bruce from the American Babylon tour.
